$schema_note: Overview definition — identity, goals, context, and high-level architecture of the Legacy IT Student Portal

system.name: Legacy IT Student Portal
system.type: Web application — React SPA frontend + Node.js REST API backend

problem_statement: Manual attendance is slow, error-prone, and easy to falsify. Faculty lack real-time visibility, students lack self-service access to their records, and admins lack accurate consolidated reports.
solution_summary: A lightweight portal where faculty display a dynamic QR code (regenerating every minute, encrypted and time-limited) that enrolled students scan to record attendance. Identity and student data come from the existing university OSAS API (single sign-on, active students only), so there is no duplicate account management. The system provides live monitoring, correction workflows, role dashboards, and exportable reports, and is architected so future portal modules plug in cleanly.
goals: Sub-5-second attendance capture per student with zero duplicate records
Single source of student identity via OSAS API integration
Modular foundation for the 10 planned future modules
non_goals_phase_1: GPS or classroom location verification (explicitly excluded)
Offline attendance capture (planned as PWA queue later)
Grades, clearance, and other future modules (separate phases)
Native mobile apps (responsive PWA-ready web instead)

stakeholders.Super Admin: IT system owner — full control, settings, audit
stakeholders.Faculty: Runs sessions, monitors and corrects attendance
stakeholders.Student: Scans QR, views records, files corrections
stakeholders.OSAS Office: External system of record for student identity and schedules (via API)

purpose: Profile sync: student_id, program, year, section, status, profile picture
Calendar exceptions: holidays, suspensions, makeup classes, special events
osas_api.direction: Server-to-server only; portal backend calls OSAS — credentials never reach the browser
osas_api.failure_mode: If OSAS is unreachable, existing sessions continue with cached profiles; new student logins are blocked with a clear message; sync retries with backoff and alerts admins

external_dependencies.object_storage: S3-compatible storage for attachments/exports in production

architecture.style: Modular monolith, feature-foldered on both tiers
architecture.diagram: React SPA (Vite) ⇄ HTTPS REST /api/v1 + Socket.IO ⇄ Express API ⇄ Prisma ⇄ PostgreSQL 16; Express ⇄ OSAS API (server-to-server); Express ⇄ SMTP / object storage

packages: packages/shared (zod schemas + TypeScript types shared by both)

key_decisions.reason: One team, one deployable, low ops cost; module boundaries enforced by folder structure and feature flags
Relational integrity for attendance constraints (unique per student+session); type-safe queries; easy migrations
Fast builds, copy-in components (no heavy UI dependency), modern accessible primitives
Server cache and UI state separated; minimal boilerplate vs Redux
Live rosters and notifications with automatic fallback to polling

key_decisions.decision: OSAS API as identity source for students
key_decisions.reason: No duplicate account management; eligibility enforced by the university system of record

non_functional_requirements.availability: 99% during class hours (07:00-21:00 Asia/Manila); realtime degrades gracefully to polling
non_functional_requirements.scalability: Sized for ~2,000 students / ~100 concurrent scans; Redis adapter + horizontal API scale when needed
non_functional_requirements.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA, full keyboard navigation, prefers-reduced-motion respected
non_functional_requirements.localization: English UI; all timestamps stored UTC, displayed in Asia/Manila
non_functional_requirements.auditability: Every auth event, override, deletion, settings change, and sync is logged with actor/IP/before/after
